name: pending-stub
description: Delivery machine quality: fix the SMART vendor-field AC guidance gap (returned from sprint 37), correct the retro first-pass formula for pre-accepted stories, and normalize themeId drift across 8 open backlog stories.
sprintGoal: Harden the delivery machine: add CRD-spec citation rule to SMART guidance, fix retro first-pass formula, and resolve backlog themeId drift.

stories.title: SMART guidance: ACs asserting vendor API field values must cite chart version CRD spec
stories.description: TEST-004b failed review three times in sprint 37 because AC3 asserted 'phase=Finished' — the documented chaos-mesh phase — but v2.6.3 uses 'Not Injected' as the terminal recovery state. The implementation was correct; the AC was wrong. Root cause: no SMART gate requires that ACs asserting specific API field values (phase names, condition strings, status keys) be cross-referenced against the pinned chart version's CRD spec or empirically verified before the story is written. Add an explicit rule to scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md: when an AC asserts a vendor-specific status field value (e.g. 'phase=X', 'condition=Y'), the story must either cite the upstream CRD documentation for the pinned chart version or note that the value was empirically confirmed against a running instance.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -n 'CRD\|vendor.*field\|phase.*value\|status.*field' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md returns at least one match confirming the guidance is present
grep -n 'chart version\|pinned version\|empirically' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md returns at least one match
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'CRD\|vendor.*field' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md; grep -n 'chart version\|empirically' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py; show all output verbatim. All three must return matches or exit 0.
stories.returnedReason: TEST-004b failed review 3x because AC3 asserted a phase name not present in the actual chart version; SMART guidance does not require CRD-spec citation for vendor API field assertions

smart.notes: All SMART dimensions green. ACs are exact grep commands against a specific file. No cluster required.

stories.title: Fix retro first-pass formula: use attempts <= 1 to include pre-accepted stories
stories.description: The retro ceremony computes first-pass rate using attempts == 1. Pre-accepted stories (review confirmations) carry attempts: 0, causing sprints composed entirely of review confirmations to report 0% pass rate. Fix: change the formula to attempts <= 1 in scripts/ralph/ceremonies/retro.md.
acceptanceCriteria: grep showing the formula in retro.md uses <= 1 rather than == 1
python3 -c "content=open('scripts/ralph/ceremonies/retro.md').read(); assert '<= 1' in content, 'formula not updated'; print('PASS')" exits 0 and prints PASS
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'attempts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/retro.md; verify the updated line uses '<= 1' and show output verbatim

smart.notes: All SMART dimensions green. Single-line formula change with exact grep ACs.

stories.title: Backlog kaizen: correct themeId drift on 8 open stories
stories.description: The following open backlog stories carry a themeId that does not match their parent epic's themeId: KIND-002 (story T2, epic E1 T1), PLATFORM-001 (story T3, epic E7 T2), PLATFORM-002 (story T1, epic E7 T2), PLATFORM-004 (story T2, epic E1 T1), PLATFORM-005 (story T3, epic E1 T1), PLATFORM-006 (story T3, epic E1 T1), QUALITY-009 (story T1, epic E12 T3), TEST-009 (story T4, epic E13 T5), TEST-010 (story T1, epic E13 T5). Update each story's themeId to match its parent epic's themeId. The correct themeId is the epic's themeId — the story's themeId should agree.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); e=json.load(open('prd/epics.json')); epic_theme={x['id']:x.get('themeId') for x in e['epics']}; drift=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if s.get('epicId') and s.get('status','open') not in ('complete','superseded') and s.get('themeId') and s['themeId'] != epic_theme.get(s['epicId'])]; assert drift==[], f'themeId drift: {drift}'; print('PASS: no themeId drift on open stories')" exits 0 and prints PASS
stories.testPlan: Run the AC one-liner verbatim and show output.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: title says '8 stories' but description lists 9 story IDs — count mismatch. Achievable score 4: AC checks ALL open stories for drift, not just the 9 listed; if additional drifted stories exist beyond those named, scope silently expands. TimeBound score 4: same reason — the AC's scope is broader than the story's enumerated list, meaning actual work may exceed what is described.
